reasoning: The USD/CAD exchange rate saw significant downward momentum in late August 2025, dropping from a peak of 1.39 to 1.3726 by August 29. While standard 30-day linear models give slightly positive probabilities due to July's upward drift, the recent heavy selloff favors short-term continuation downward. Macroeconomic consensus, including a July 2025 Reuters poll of 70 FX analysts and updated RBC currency analysis, indicates the U.S. dollar will likely remain weak over the remainder of the year due to tariff concerns and US debt issues. Specifically, major Canadian banks forecast USD/CAD to drift lower towards 1.35 and eventually 1.31 by the end of 2026, driven by UIRP dynamics and expectations that BoC rate cuts will stimulate the Canadian economy. These forecasts suggest a gradual decline in the USD/CAD exchange rate across the medium and long term, meaning an increase above the late-August value is less than 50% likely. In strict adherence to the mandate, uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, pulling distant date probabilities progressively closer to 0.5 (from below).
